hero.title: Ease Healthcare
hero.lead: A storefront designed to make a sensitive category feel calm, clinical and trustworthy — turning hesitation into confidence and browsers into repeat buyers.

brief.label: The brief
brief.p1: Ease Healthcare sits in a category most shoppers approach quietly. The goal wasn't just to sell pH-balanced intimate wellness products — it was to remove friction and embarrassment, and replace it with the reassurance of a brand that clearly knows what it's doing.
brief.p2: The existing presence felt scattered: strong products, but no single place that told the brand's story or guided a first-time buyer with confidence. I set out to build a storefront that reads as clinical but human — credible enough to trust, warm enough to feel safe.

idea.label: The idea
idea.p1: The concept was "quiet confidence." A soft, skin-adjacent palette and generous whitespace lower the emotional temperature, while precise typography and science-backed messaging do the reassuring. Nothing shouts; everything earns trust.
idea.p2: Every module answers one unspoken question a shopper has — Is this for me? Is it safe? Will it work? — so the path from curiosity to checkout feels effortless. Hence the name: Ease.

notes.title: Hero banner
notes.body: A calm, full-width opener that states the brand promise in one line. The product sits in clean negative space so the first impression is composure, not clutter — setting the tone for everything below.

notes.title: Brand promise & credibility
notes.body: Short, scannable proof points — pH-balanced, dermatologically considered, made for real bodies. This is where hesitation turns into trust, so the copy stays factual and the layout stays uncrowded.

notes.title: Product showcase
notes.body: Each hero product gets room to breathe with consistent mockups and a unified light direction, so the range reads as one considered family rather than separate listings.

notes.title: Education module
notes.body: A simple "how & why it works" section that pre-answers the questions shoppers are too shy to search. Diagrams and plain language do the heavy lifting — informative, never clinical-cold.

notes.title: Social proof
notes.body: Real reassurance — reviews and results framed in the brand's soft visual language so they feel like part of the story, not a bolted-on widget.

notes.title: Closing call-to-action
notes.body: A warm, low-pressure nudge to shop the full range, with navigation that loops back to the hero so the browsing experience never dead-ends.

outcome.label: The outcome
outcome.h2: A space that does the reassuring for you.
outcome.p1: The finished storefront gives Ease a single, cohesive home on Amazon — one that educates, builds trust, and guides shoppers toward the right product without pressure.
outcome.p2: Add your results here — e.g. uplift in conversion, time-on-store, or units sold once you have the numbers. Concrete outcomes make the strongest close to a case study.

optiflow.banner: Draft copy — edit this case study's text and images in the admin at /admin/ → Case Studies. The Premium A+ layout loads from images/Optiflow.png.

hero.title: Optiflow
hero.lead: A Premium A+ brand story that uses Amazon's largest canvas to its full advantage — edge-to-edge visuals, interactive hotspots and video that turn a detail page into an immersive, objection-proof experience.

brief.label: The brief
brief.p1: Optiflow had earned the brand-registry status to unlock Premium A+ — Amazon's top content tier — but a bigger canvas only helps if the design actually uses it. Standard modules dropped into a premium slot just look like standard modules with more whitespace.
brief.p2: The brief was to build a true Premium experience: full-width imagery, interactive hotspots, a hero video and a navigable comparison table — a detail page that feels less like a listing and more like a flagship microsite living inside Amazon.

idea.label: The idea
idea.p1: Premium A+ removes the gutters, so I designed edge-to-edge — letting imagery run full-bleed and using scale itself to signal that Optiflow is a premium brand before a word is read.
idea.p2: Every premium-only module earns its place: hotspots reveal detail on demand, the video carries the product in motion, and the comparison table lets shoppers self-select — so the page does the selling interactively instead of asking anyone to read a wall of text.

anatomy.label: Anatomy of the Premium A+ page

notes.title: Full-bleed brand hero
notes.body: Premium A+ unlocks an edge-to-edge banner, so the page opens on a cinematic, full-width statement of what Optiflow is and who it's for — scale doing the premium positioning before any copy.

notes.title: Interactive hotspots
notes.body: A premium-only module where shoppers hover or tap points on the product to reveal feature callouts. It turns a static spec list into exploration — answering "what does this do?" the moment it's asked.

notes.title: Hero video
notes.body: An auto-playing video module — exclusive to Premium A+ — shows the product in motion and in context. Seeing it work removes the doubt that still images leave behind.

notes.title: Image carousel
notes.body: A swipeable gallery packs lifestyle shots, detail crops and use-cases into a single slot — depth without forcing an endless scroll, and a layout that feels native on mobile.

notes.title: Comparison table
notes.body: An enhanced cross-sell table maps the range side by side so a shopper can self-select the right Optiflow for them — lifting basket size while genuinely helping them decide.

notes.title: FAQ & brand close
notes.body: An accordion FAQ pre-empts the last objections, then a closing brand panel loops the story back to the promise in the hero — so the experience resolves instead of just ending.

outcome.label: The outcome
outcome.p1: The finished Premium A+ story gives Optiflow a detail page with the polish of a standalone brand site — interactive, full-canvas and built to convert the shoppers who research before they buy.
outcome.p2: Add your results here — e.g. conversion-rate uplift, engagement on the interactive modules, or units sold once you have the numbers. Concrete outcomes make the strongest close to a case study.

dermtheory.banner: Draft copy — edit this case study's text and images in the admin at /admin/ → Case Studies. The six visuals load from images/dermtheory-1.jpg … -6.jpg.

hero.title: Dermtheory
hero.lead: A set of detail-page infographics that turn a busy listing into a calm, clinical brand story — pre-answering objections, proving the science, and carrying the shopper all the way to "add to cart".

brief.label: The brief
brief.p1: Dermtheory is clinical skincare in a category overflowing with claims. The stock detail page did the basics — a title, a few bullets, a gallery — but it left every real question unanswered: What's actually in it? Is it backed by anything? Will it work for my skin?
brief.p2: The brief was to build an infographic set that does the convincing the listing copy can't — a sequence of visuals that reads less like an advert and more like a confident, evidence-led explainer. Credible enough to trust, clean enough to scan in seconds.

idea.label: The idea
idea.p1: I treated the set as a narrative with a deliberate sequence — hook, problem, science, routine, results, range. Each visual earns the next scroll by resolving one doubt before the shopper has to think it.
idea.p2: A restrained clinical palette, consistent grid and a single light direction across every mockup make the range read as one considered system — so the visuals feel like the brand, not a template dropped onto the page.

notes.title: Brand banner
notes.body: A full-width opener that establishes Dermtheory as a clinical authority in one line. Logo, promise and a clean product hero set the tone before a single claim is made — credibility first, sell later.

notes.title: The problem
notes.body: Names the exact skin concern the shopper is here to solve, framed in plain language. Putting their problem on the page in their words is what earns the rest of the scroll.

notes.title: Ingredient science
notes.body: The hero actives, what each one does, and why the formulation works — diagrams and concise callouts instead of a wall of text. This is where a "maybe" turns into a "this is legit".

notes.title: How to use
notes.body: A simple, numbered routine that shows exactly where the product fits in a daily regimen — removing the friction of "how do I even use this" before it becomes a reason not to buy.

notes.title: Results & proof
notes.body: Before-and-after framing and trust signals presented in the brand's own visual language, so the evidence reads as part of the story rather than a bolted-on claim.

notes.title: The range
notes.body: A comparison visual that cross-sells the rest of the line — helping the shopper find their match and lifting basket size without ever feeling like a hard upsell.

outcome.label: The outcome
outcome.p1: The finished set gives Dermtheory a detail page that does more than describe the product — it builds the case for it, visual by visual, and answers the objections that quietly cost conversions.
outcome.p2: Add your results here — e.g. conversion-rate uplift, lower return rate, or units sold once you have the numbers. Concrete outcomes make the strongest close to a case study.

hero.title: Moonjax
hero.lead: A warm, lifestyle-led storefront designed to make everyday home products feel desirable — cohesive, considered and easy to shop.

brief.label: The brief
brief.p1: Moonjax needed a storefront that turns a range of home & lifestyle products into a single, aspirational brand world — somewhere shoppers want to linger, not just land and leave.
brief.p2: Add the real context here — who the brand is, the product range, and the goal of the store.

idea.label: The idea
idea.p1: Warm tones, lifestyle imagery and clean modular sections give the store a homely yet premium feel — products shown in context so shoppers can picture them in their own space.
idea.p2: Swap in your actual concept — palette, imagery direction and the thinking behind it.

outcome.label: The outcome
outcome.h2: A brand world that feels like home.
outcome.p1: The result is a cohesive, inviting storefront that gives Moonjax a premium presence and a smooth path from browsing to basket.

hero.title: Beemo
hero.lead: A vibrant, social storefront designed to make drinkware and barware feel fun and giftable — bright, energetic and built to convert.

brief.label: The brief
brief.p1: Beemo needed a storefront with personality — one that captures the fun, social energy of drinkware and barware and makes the range feel like the perfect gift or treat.
brief.p2: Add the real context here — the brand, the products, and what the store needed to do.

idea.label: The idea
idea.p1: Bright color, playful imagery and confident typography give the store an upbeat, social mood — products styled in moments of celebration so shoppers feel the occasion.
idea.p2: Swap in your actual concept — palette, imagery direction and the rationale.

outcome.label: The outcome
outcome.p1: The finished storefront gives Beemo a lively, memorable presence on Amazon and a clear, enjoyable path from browsing to checkout.
outcome.p2: Add results here once available — conversion, engagement or sales impact.
